questions.question: A basketball team scored $3$ points for every $5$ points scored by their opponents. What is the ratio of points scored by the team to points scored by their opponents?
A cyclist travels $12$ miles in $2$ hours. If she travels at the same rate, how many miles will she travel in $5$ hours?
A football team has a record of $8$ wins to $2$ losses. What is the ratio of wins to losses?
A tennis player hits $9$ aces out of every $15$ serves. What is the ratio of aces to serves?
A marathon runner runs $18$ miles in $3$ hours. If she runs at the same rate, how many miles will she run in $4$ hours?
A swimmer swims $200$ meters in $4$ minutes. What is the ratio of meters swum to minutes taken?
A gymnast performs $12$ routines out of $20$ attempts. What is the ratio of successful routines to attempts?
A baseball player gets $8$ hits out of every $15$ at-bats. What is the ratio of hits to at-bats?

A sprinter runs $100$ meters in $10$ seconds. If she runs at the same rate, how many meters will she run in $t$ seconds? Express your answer as a proportion and solve for $t$ when she runs $200$ meters.
A volleyball team wins $3$ out of every $5$ games. If they play $20$ games, how many games will they win? Express your answer as a proportion and solve for the number of games won.

teacher_rules: Ensure students understand the concept of ratios and proportions
Remind students to simplify ratios and proportions whenever possible
Encourage students to use real-world examples to illustrate their understanding of ratios and proportions
